programme.title: The Health Of Our Oceans
programme.desc: Renowned marine biologist Dr. Sylvia Earle reveals why the dual threats of ocean pollution and overfishing could have a devastating impact on mankind.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: As scientific studies confirm sea level changes throughout the globe, major coastal cities like Miami are now fighting back against these rising tides.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: Hundreds of millions of people around the world could run out of water soon. Experts are desperately searching for solutions to stave off "Day Zero" when the taps will run dry. But the recent discovery of a massive aquifer hidden beneath the U.S. coastline may offer the greatest hope for humanity.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: The real impact of global warming is now being felt in communities around the world. Climate scientists explain what to expect where you live.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: After record-setting conflagrations erupted in the region’s prized forests, new tools using cutting edge technology are being developed to help save property and lives.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: The Greenland ice sheet, the last remnant of the Ice Age, is melting at an unprecedented rate. Today, scientists and researchers from all over the world are paying close attention to what could become a global catastrophe.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: Learn how the history of forest management and loss of public trust, coupled with climate change, have led to the conditions driving mega-fire nearly year-round.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: A team of scientists embarks on a quest to explore the mesopelagic zone or ocean twilight zone which sits beneath the surface layer of the ocean.

programme.title: Climate Impact Asia
programme.desc: By 2100 over 1 billion people on Earth will live in low-lying coastal zones. 70% of these will live in Southeast Asia, a highly vulnerable region to climate change.

programme.title: Climate Impact Asia
programme.desc: Natural hazards remain a constant challenge for over 400 million people who live in low lying communities built along major mega delta regions in coastal zones.

programme.title: Climate Impact Asia
programme.desc: Many people now know that climate change is real, that they see the data or they see the observations for themselves. What's needed now is a sense of urgency.

programme.title: Climate Impact Asia
programme.desc: Rice, it's a staple food for many in Asia. It provides 1/3 of the energy in the average Asian diet and is the income source for more than 500 million people.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Kelp forests harbor a greater variety and higher diversity of plants and animals than almost any other ocean community. Many organisms use the thick blades as a safe shelter for their young from predators or even rough storms. But, what can be done when this ecosystem is out of balance.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Antarctica is a mysterious and unexplored continent. Thanks to the Chilean Antarctic Institute and the research center Dynamics of Marine Ecosystems of High Latitudes, a group of scientists are suffering the extreme temperatures to study climate change and the earth's ocean currents.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: More than 60,000 fires scorched the Amazon in 2019 causing enormous damage to an ecosystem that’s vital to human existence. What caused the devastating blazes, how will they impact the climate crisis and will the Amazon ever be able to recover.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: From wildflower blooms in the desert to large colonies of jellyfish off of Monterey and unbelievable glow in the dark waves, this is California at its finest. Life can bloom in the most unexpected of ways.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Tokyo is the most populated urban zone on the planet. Built on a fault line, threatened by flooding and several active volcanoes, the city seems doomed to disappear.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: The SUrge-STructure-Atmosphere INteraction facility, or SUSTAIN, will help scientists at the Rosenstiel School of Marine and Atmospheric Science better understand the complex interactions between a hurricane and the ocean below it, as well as the coastlines it may affect.

programme.title: The Health Of Our Oceans
programme.desc: Renowned marine biologist Dr. Sylvia Earle reveals why the dual threats of ocean pollution and overfishing could have a devastating impact on mankind.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: As scientific studies confirm sea level changes throughout the globe, major coastal cities like Miami are now fighting back against these rising tides.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: Hundreds of millions of people around the world could run out of water soon. Experts are desperately searching for solutions to stave off "Day Zero" when the taps will run dry. But the recent discovery of a massive aquifer hidden beneath the U.S. coastline may offer the greatest hope for humanity.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: The real impact of global warming is now being felt in communities around the world. Climate scientists explain what to expect where you live.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: After record-setting conflagrations erupted in the region’s prized forests, new tools using cutting edge technology are being developed to help save property and lives.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: The Greenland ice sheet, the last remnant of the Ice Age, is melting at an unprecedented rate. Today, scientists and researchers from all over the world are paying close attention to what could become a global catastrophe.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: Learn how the history of forest management and loss of public trust, coupled with climate change, have led to the conditions driving mega-fire nearly year-round.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: A team of scientists embarks on a quest to explore the mesopelagic zone or ocean twilight zone which sits beneath the surface layer of the ocean.

programme.title: Climate Impact Asia
programme.desc: By 2100 over 1 billion people on Earth will live in low-lying coastal zones. 70% of these will live in Southeast Asia, a highly vulnerable region to climate change.

programme.title: Climate Impact Asia
programme.desc: Natural hazards remain a constant challenge for over 400 million people who live in low lying communities built along major mega delta regions in coastal zones.

programme.title: Climate Impact Asia
programme.desc: Many people now know that climate change is real, that they see the data or they see the observations for themselves. What's needed now is a sense of urgency.

programme.title: Climate Impact Asia
programme.desc: Rice, it's a staple food for many in Asia. It provides 1/3 of the energy in the average Asian diet and is the income source for more than 500 million people.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: As Europe recovered from the damage of WWII, soldiers made their way home from the front line. But one man brought back all the savagery and brutality of the fighting.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: John Christie brought death to everyone around him. He managed to pin his terrible crimes on another man_who would be sentenced to death.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: John Christie brought death to everyone around him. He managed to pin his terrible crimes on another man who would be sentenced to death. He eluded capture for years.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: On a hot summer's day in 1934, a horrifying discovery was made in the trunk at the luggage office of Brighton Train Station. Two days later, a similar case was found.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: As police investigated the disappearance of a wealthy widow, Olive Durand Deacon, in early 1949, they could not have imagined the gruesome confession told to them.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: As Europe recovered from the damage of WWII, soldiers made their way home from the front line. But one man brought back all the savagery and brutality of the fighting.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: John Christie brought death to everyone around him. He managed to pin his terrible crimes on another man_who would be sentenced to death.

programme.title: Murder Maps

programme.desc: On a hot summer's day in 1934, a horrifying discovery was made in the trunk at the luggage office of Brighton Train Station. Two days later, a similar case was found.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: As police investigated the disappearance of a wealthy widow, Olive Durand Deacon, in early 1949, they could not have imagined the gruesome confession told to them.

programme.desc: As police investigated the disappearance of a wealthy widow, Olive Durand Deacon, in early 1949, they could not have imagined the gruesome confession told to them.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: John Christie brought death to everyone around him. He managed to pin his terrible crimes on another man_who would be sentenced to death.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: John Christie brought death to everyone around him. He managed to pin his terrible crimes on another man who would be sentenced to death. He eluded capture for years.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: On a hot summer's day in 1934, a horrifying discovery was made in the trunk at the luggage office of Brighton Train Station. Two days later, a similar case was found.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: As police investigated the disappearance of a wealthy widow, Olive Durand Deacon, in early 1949, they could not have imagined the gruesome confession told to them.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: As Europe recovered from the damage of WWII, soldiers made their way home from the front line. But one man brought back all the savagery and brutality of the fighting.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: John Christie brought death to everyone around him. He managed to pin his terrible crimes on another man_who would be sentenced to death.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Top trappers from Florida Fish and Wildlife go to battle each day against dangerous nuisance alligators that lurk beneath the lush lakes and canals of Palm Beach.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Strap-in for a breathtaking tour of the history and wildlife of Lake Okeechobee, one of the largest freshwater lakes in the United States, and the source of life for South Florida's magnificent Everglades.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Should we save parasites? These species have a bad reputation but are an essential part of healthy ecosystems throughout the world.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Growing up in Montana, Casey Anderson came to love and appreciate wild animals at a very young age. He found a particular kinship with bears. One day he was faced with a fateful decision, leave and orphaned grizzly cub to die in the wild or save it. Now he is faced with that same decision again.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Follow a family of sperm whales in the heart of the Indian Ocean. Alongside a team of scientists, who have witnessed their most intimate moments over the past 7 years, we will lift the veil on the largest toothed predator on earth.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Meet a family of Chungungos, the world's mysterious, smallest otter. Living along the untamed coast of the southern Pacific Ocean, these furry, intelligent, and adorable creatures make their home in the wild of Chile.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Take a journey with famed underwater photographer Peter Kragh, as he explores the coast of California, and reveals some of the most spectacular life found under the sea.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Join ecologist Karen Odendaal as she gives us a tour of the Manyoni Private Game Reserve in the heart of Zululand, South Africa.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Throw on your wetsuit, and join filmmakers Kyle McBurnie and Alex Warham as they show off their California wild backyard, deep in the Pacific Ocean.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Take a trip to the north of Mexico with our host, Eréndira Hernández, as she shows off one of the most extraordinary landscapes in the Americas.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: The hustle and bustle of New York City is legendary worldwide, but right in its backyard lies a serene natural haven that most know very little about.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Meet Rene Araneda, a wildlife filmmaker and Patagonia explorer, as he takes you on a tour of his favorite place in the world: Torres del Paine.

programme.title: Changing Ocean Asia
programme.desc: In this episode, travel with scientists from the Earth Observatory of Singapore across the region to understand what is causing cities to sink.

programme.title: Changing Ocean Asia
programme.desc: For scientists at the Earth Observatory of Singapore, it is a race against time to understand the effects climate change will have on coastal infrastructures.

programme.title: Changing Ocean Asia
programme.desc: Dive with scientists from the Earth Observatory of Singapore as they investigate the impact climate change is having on the ocean.

programme.title: Changing Ocean Asia
programme.sub-title: Sea Level Rise and Engineering Solutions
programme.desc: South-east Asia is a unique part of the world, featuring low-lying countries, pristine islands, and vast coastal habitats.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Top trappers from Florida Fish and Wildlife go to battle each day against dangerous nuisance alligators that lurk beneath the lush lakes and canals of Palm Beach.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Strap-in for a breathtaking tour of the history and wildlife of Lake Okeechobee, one of the largest freshwater lakes in the United States, and the source of life for South Florida's magnificent Everglades.
